purpose: Compact grounding data for an AI Bidding Coach that drafts and explains NAVBLUE/PBS bid strategy for Delta pilots.

sources.title: Delta Pilots' PBS Reference Handbook v4

concepts.label: Top-down seniority processing
concepts.summary: PBS processes pilots in seniority order. The available pairing pool shrinks as more senior pilots are awarded lines.

concepts.label: Bid groups are independent attempts
concepts.summary: PBS remains in a bid group until the group builds an award, an exit instruction moves processing to the next group, or the pilot cannot hold that type of line.

concepts.label: Conditional versus unconditional bid groups
concepts.summary: Bid groups with Else Start Next or Clear Schedule and Start Next are conditional. Without exits, a pairing group ultimately accepts any legal regular line PBS can build.

concepts.label: Negative preferences filter before awards
concepts.summary: Prefer Off and Avoid Pairings remove trips from consideration; Award Pairings then tries to place trips from the remaining pool.

concepts.label: Line Construction Window
concepts.summary: Regular lines are built inside the LCW around the ALV, subject to absences, FAR limits, PWA limits, thresholds, and coverage.

concepts.label: Coverage risk
concepts.summary: Coverage can cause otherwise valid preferences to be denied to satisfy staffing needs, especially around holidays or for junior bidders.

bidPreferenceTemplates.label: Award Pairings
bidPreferenceTemplates.draftPattern: Award Pairings If [pairing property/date/route/layover/credit condition] [optional Limit N]

bidPreferenceTemplates.label: Avoid Pairings
useWhen: Deadheads, redeyes, trip lengths, or fatigue drivers

bidPreferenceTemplates.label: Set Condition credit window
bidPreferenceTemplates.draftPattern: Set Condition [Minimum Credit | Maximum Credit | Mid Credit]
useWhen: Balancing pay and quality of life

bidPreferenceTemplates.label: Else Start Next Bid Group
useWhen: A preference is a hard boundary
Pilot wants fallback instead of denied preference

bidPreferenceTemplates.label: Clear Schedule and Start Next Bid Group
bidPreferenceTemplates.draftPattern: Clear Schedule and Start Next Bid Group
useWhen: Avoid system-generated completion or denied negatives

coachRules: Interview before drafting when goals are ambiguous.
Ground draft recommendations in the loaded bid package and pilot seniority.
Do not promise awards without simulator support.
Never call an award, day off, or pairing result guaranteed.
Label Phase 0 bid text as a review-ready starting draft, not copy-and-paste-ready output.
Do not add waivers unless the pilot explicitly confirms that tradeoff.
Prefer a narrow ideal group followed by broader fallback groups.
